text: Good evening. Let’s take a clear, honest look at your day.
How would you describe your day overall?
options: Productive|Challenging|Mixed|Frustrating
answer=Productive|Mixed:A1_Q1_HIGH;answer=Challenging|Frustrating:A1_Q1_LOW

text: When things worked today, what made it happen?
options: My preparation|Team support|Luck|I adapted quickly

text: When things didn’t go well, what was your instinct?
options: Fix what I could|Wait for help|Push through blindly|Felt stuck

text: In a difficult moment, what did you actually do?
options: Adjusted approach|Asked for clarity|Kept going same way|Ignored the issue

text: In that moment, what felt most true?
options: I had some control|Others needed to act|Nothing would help|I avoided it
answer=Adjusted approach|Asked for clarity:A1_R_INT;answer=Kept going same way|Ignored the issue:A1_R_MIX
answer=I had some control:A1_R_INT;answer=Others needed to act|Nothing would help|I avoided it:A1_R_EXT

text: You recognized your influence today. Even small adjustments count — that’s agency in action.
There were moments you could have shaped more. Noticing that is where control begins.
Today felt driven by external factors. Still, somewhere you made a call — finding that is key.
Now shift focus — from what happened to what you contributed.
Let’s move from outcomes to effort — what you gave today.
Let’s step away from circumstances and look at your contribution.
Think of one interaction today. What stands out most?
options: I helped someone|I did my work|I needed support|I felt overlooked

text: What best describes your effort today?
options: Went beyond role|Did what was required|Held back effort|Expected recognition
answer=Went beyond role:A2_R_HIGH;answer=Did what was required:A2_R_NEUTRAL;answer=Held back effort|Expected recognition:A2_R_LOW

text: You contributed beyond obligation. That builds trust and long-term value.
You met expectations. There’s room to stretch into meaningful contribution.
Your focus leaned toward what you didn’t receive. That can quietly limit impact.
Why did you go beyond?
options: It felt right|Team needed it|I take pride|Wanted recognition

text: What frustrated you most?
options: Lack of recognition|Others didn’t help|Work felt unfair|Too much expected
answer=It felt right|Team needed it|I take pride:A2_R2_HIGH;answer=Wanted recognition:A2_R2_MIX
answer=Lack of recognition|Work felt unfair:A2_R2_LOW;answer=Others didn’t help|Too much expected:A2_R2_MIX

text: You acted from ownership, not reward. That’s real contribution.
There’s a mix — contribution and expectation. Noticing that balance matters.
Your attention stayed on what was missing. Shifting to what you can give changes the game.
Now zoom out — beyond you — to the bigger picture.
Let’s widen the lens — who else was affected today?
Let’s step beyond yourself — and look outward.
When you think about today’s challenges, who comes to mind?
options: Just me|My team|A colleague|Customer/end user

text: What shaped your decisions most today?
options: My goals|Team needs|Helping someone|User impact
answer=My goals:A3_Q2_SELF;answer=Team needs:A3_Q2_TEAM;answer=Helping someone|User impact:A3_Q2_WIDE

text: What might you have overlooked?
options: Team struggles|Someone needed help|User impact|Nothing

text: How did your actions affect the team?
options: Helped progress|Neutral impact|Added pressure|Unsure

text: What drove your outward focus?
options: Empathy|Responsibility|Experience|Habit

text: Your focus stayed close to yourself. Expanding it could ease pressure and reveal new meaning.
You considered the team. That broader view strengthens collaboration and trust.
You thought beyond yourself — toward others. That’s where deeper meaning and impact grow.
Today you leaned {axis1.dominant} on control, showed {axis2.dominant} in contribution, and focused {axis3.dominant}. These patterns shape how your day unfolds — and how tomorrow can improve.
That’s it for today. See you tomorrow.
